body:

```
[2026-04-17T17:58:56.441Z] [INFO]     "mcp__claude_ai_Gmail__authenticate",
[2026-04-17T17:58:56.441Z] [INFO]     "mcp__claude_ai_Gmail__complete_authentication",
[2026-04-17T17:58:56.441Z] [INFO]     "mcp__claude_ai_Google_Calendar__authenticate",

[2026-04-17T17:58:56.441Z] [INFO]     "mcp__claude_ai_Google_Drive__authenticate",
[2026-04-17T17:58:56.441Z] [INFO]     "mcp__claude_ai_Google_Drive__complete_authentication",
```

These 3 MCP servers should be turned off, as for our use case it is impossible to use them.

```
  Planning & Tracking                                                                                                                                                      
  - EnterPlanMode / ExitPlanMode — Read-only mode. Restricts me to reads until you approve or reject a plan. Only writable file is the plan at ~/.claude/plans/<name>.md.
  Requires user approval to enter and to exit.                                                                                                                             
  - EnterWorktree / ExitWorktree — Isolated git worktree under .claude/worktrees/. Only triggered when "worktree" is explicitly mentioned. ExitWorktree takes action:    
  "keep" or "remove".                                                                                                                                                      
  - AskUserQuestion — Blocking multiple-choice prompt. Not usable in autonomous mode (needs a human to click).                                                             
   
  Scheduling & Automation                                                                                                                                                  
  - ScheduleWakeup — Self-paced /loop only. Single delay, 60–3600s. Dies with session.                                                                                   
  - CronCreate / CronList / CronDelete — NOT remote agents. They're local session cron jobs: 5-field cron, recurring or one-shot, auto-expire after 7 days, session-scoped 
  (or durable: true to persist to disk). My earlier "remote agents" description was wrong.                                                                                
  - RemoteTrigger — This is the actual remote one. Wraps claude.ai trigger API (list/get/create/update/run). OAuth injected automatically.                                 
  - PushNotification — Desktop notification + mobile (if Remote Control connected). Suppressed when terminal has focus — so switch windows or sleep via Bash before firing
  to actually see it. Useful in autonomous mode.     
```

All these tools should be disabled, as they will not have any value in our workflow (autonomous headless execution on server without UI), so most of them will just eat token budget with no benefit, they can also do harm by making system unpredictable or stuck it completely due to wait for user reaction that will never come. Or some of these tools require system modification on global scale or usage of additional accounts.

This is full exact list of tools for reference (disable only what I listed before that):

The commands that we target to be disabled should be disabled in docker up on installation, and solve command on each run should verify if they are disabled or not, and if somehow enabled - solve command should automatically disable them before attempting to execute claude command inside.

We need to download all logs and data related about the issue to this repository, make sure we compile that data to `./docs/case-studies/issue-{id}` folder, and use it to do deep case study analysis (also make sure to search online for additional facts and data), in which we will reconstruct timeline/sequence of events, list of each and all requirements from the issue, find root causes of the each problem, and propose possible solutions and solution plans for each requirement (we should also check known existing components/libraries, that solve similar problem or can help in solutions).

If there is not enough data to find actual root cause, add debug output and verbose mode if not present, that will allow us to find root cause on next iteration.

If issue related to any other repository/project, where we can report issues on GitHub, please do so. Each issue must contain reproducible examples, workarounds and suggestions for fix the issue in code.
title: Disable useless tools and MCP servers for Claude Code (in docker + solve command level, better both)
